violations.text: Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of biofilm and soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Bagged ice will be used until ice machine has been cleaned and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Day-old bagels packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Bagels were placed behind the counter instead of self-service area. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen prep unit beneath the lid have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration in the 3 bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (floor mixer, and knives in the knife rack) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Mixer and knives are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Cut tomatoes, turkey, and chicken observed held at 48-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the sandwich prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed in walk in cooler. Discussed with PIC not overfilling food containers in prep unit, using metal containers, and not placing stacked plastic containers into containers on prep unit, which prevents cold from reaching food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Specialty Bagels (Cheddar & jalapeno, and spinach & garlic) observed held at 66 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment will request to use Time as Public Health Control for specialty bagels. Request form and log given to PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm) in the sanitizer bucket. A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Specialty cream cheeses packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. English Food Label Approval Form English Food Labeling for Packaged Food New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Sponge observed at the 3 bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Scoop in dry ingredients observed stored with the handle in the product. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Water observed in the blender bowl.After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Single service items observed stored on shelves less than 6 inches above the floor in the dry storage area.Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Paper towels observed stored in the toilet room on the floor, and uncovered on a shelf above the toilet.Cleaned and SANITIZED EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, laundered LINENS, and SINGLESERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES may not be stored:(1)In locker rooms;(2)In toilet rooms;(3)In garbage rooms;(4)In mechanical rooms;(5)Under sewer lines that are not shielded to interceptpotential drips;(6)Under leaking water lines including leaking automatic fire sprinkler heads or under lines on which water has condensed;(7)Under open stairwells; or(8)Under other sources of contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
The floor mixer was not in good repair, with a panel missing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer, slicer, prep unit, ingredient bins, portable fan, rolling racks, have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris.Numerous food containers observed with an accumulation of paper & adhesive residue. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
The walk in fan covers & ceiling have an accumulation of dust and mold. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer adjusted during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
Hole observed in dry storage room. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
